data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T213639-53d84f93/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T213639-53d84f93/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T213639-53d84f93/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T213639-53d84f93/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T213639-53d84f93/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="fizeau-b5757624">
    <title>routing: bound TCP dial timeout to ~5s, not Go default ~30s</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
A dead local provider endpoint costs ~30 seconds of wall-clock per call before Fizeau surfaces the dial error. The fizeau-8134d6db AC required dial-class failures to bail in under 5 seconds; the shipped implementation only short-circuits the *retry loop* (one attempt instead of four), but the *dial itself* still uses Go's default TCP connect timeout (~30s on Linux). Observed today: every bead routed to bragi:1234 / bragi:8020 / grendel:1234 / grendel:1235 / grendel:8000 took ~30s to fail with "dial tcp ... i/o timeout". With 14 beads in the ddx queue all routing to a single dead host, the drain spent ~7 minutes establishing that the host was dead — meeting fizeau-8134d6db's letter (single attempt) while missing its spirit (under 5s).

ROOT CAUSE
internal/core/loop.go:643 (IsDialError short-circuit) is correctly wired. The retry-loop layer fails fast.

What is NOT bounded: the underlying http.Transport's DialContext. internal/provider/openai/openai.go and internal/sdk/openaicompat construct openaicompat.Config without overriding the HTTP client's transport. Go's net/http default Transport has no explicit DialContext, so it uses net.Dialer{} with Timeout=0 — which on Linux waits for the kernel's full TCP SYN retransmit timeout (≈21s + RTO backoff = 30s observed). For a known-local endpoint that the operator just unplugged, 30s is pure waste.

PROPOSED FIX
- Configure an explicit net.Dialer with Timeout=5s (or ServiceOptions-driven, defaulting to 5s) in the openaicompat client construction path.
- Wrap http.Transport.DialContext so the connect phase is bounded independent of the request streaming timeout.
- Keep the request-streaming timeout (Timeout field) separate — large requests legitimately take minutes once connected.
- Expose ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config so callers (DDx, benchmarks) can tune for high-latency networks if needed; default 5s.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the retry-loop short-circuit (already correct).
- Do not change in-flight 5xx retry behavior.
- Do not bound the request body / streaming timeout in this bead.

DEPS
None.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutDefault5s asserts that the openaicompat.NewClient default Transport has an explicit DialContext with timeout ≤5s.
2. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutConfigurable asserts ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config overrides the default.
3. TestProviderDeadLocalHost_FailsUnder5s — integration test: aim at an unroutable RFC 5737 address (e.g., 192.0.2.1:1) and assert the provider call returns within 5.5s with a dial error. (No process listening means kernel-level connect timeout exercises the bounded dial.)
4. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
5. CHANGELOG.md notes the new ConnectTimeout default + tunable.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:provider, kind:bug</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="6dbd81cec37d14901aa3b25a5b07e8b655229916" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T213639-53d84f93"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
